name: Dental Records & Consent (GDC aligned)
description: Reference policy aligned with GDC Standards for the Dental Team and FGDP record-keeping guidance. Documentation is the primary defence in GDC complaints — the policy emphasises completeness.

data.text: Records are full, accurate, and contemporaneous — sufficient for another dental professional to continue care and to defend a complaint.
Adult records are retained for at least 11 years after the last contact.
Records for patients under 18 are retained until age 25, or 11 years after last contact, whichever is later.
Tooth charting uses an explicit notation system; restorations, caries, mobility, and missing teeth are recorded at every full exam.
Basic Periodontal Examination (BPE) is recorded at every full exam; full periodontal charting is recorded when indicated.
Informed consent is documented (Montgomery-style) — risks, benefits, alternatives, and the patient's stated decision.
Records form the practice's defence in GDC fitness-to-practise proceedings; gaps are treated as standards-of-care concerns.

clauses.title: Full, accurate, contemporaneous
clauses.body: Records must be full, accurate, and contemporaneous — sufficient for another professional to continue care and to defend a complaint.

clauses.title: 11-year adult retention
clauses.body: Adult records must be retained at least 11 years from last contact.

clauses.title: Child retention to age 25
clauses.body: Records for patients under 18 must be retained until 25 or 11 years from last contact, whichever is later.

clauses.title: Tooth charting recorded
clauses.body: Every full exam must include tooth charting using an explicit notation system.

clauses.title: BPE recorded
clauses.body: Every full exam must record a Basic Periodontal Examination.

clauses.title: Montgomery-style consent
clauses.body: Informed consent must be documented covering risks, benefits, alternatives, and the patient's decision.

clauses.title: Records support FtP defence
clauses.body: Records are the practice's defence in GDC fitness-to-practise proceedings; gaps are treated as standards-of-care concerns.
